Never fabricated (G2): built either"] # [doc = " trivially (the whole phylum checked clean) or by the import-closure sub-phylum re-check — always"] # [doc = " grounded in a real `check_phylum` run, never guessed."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq)] pub enum NoduleClass { # [doc = " *N*'s whole transitive import closure checked clean (the DN-124 §2.1 soundness invariant)."] Clean , # [doc = " *N*'s own closure sub-phylum failed, attributed to *N* itself — either the failure's site was"] # [doc = " resolved to *N*'s own nodule path, or the attribution was **not cleanly recoverable** from the"] # [doc = " evidence (most check-error sites are bare item names, not nodule-qualified — VR-5: never claim"] # [doc = " a finer `Blocked` class than the evidence supports; report the weaker class instead)."] CheckError { # [doc = " The failure's site, as reported by the kernel checker."] site : String , # [doc = " The failure's message, as reported by the kernel checker."] message : String , } , # [doc = " *N*'s own closure sub-phylum failed, and the failure was **confidently** attributed (via a"] # [doc = " nodule-qualified site, e.g. an unresolved `<use>`) to a **different** nodule in *N*'s import"] # [doc = " closure — named explicitly, never a silent \"it's someone's fault\" (G2)."] Blocked { # [doc = " The dotted path of the closure member the failure was attributed to."] on : String , # [doc = " The failure's message, as reported by the kernel checker."] message : String , } , }
gaps.reason: enum `NoduleClass` derive(Eq): covered by co-occurring PartialEq → `fn eq_NoduleClass` (product-row collision policy; DN-128 / ONESHOT C2) — not a second emission

(self , NoduleClass :: Clean) } }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A per-nodule verdict. gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The result of checking a set of sources **as one phylum**."] # [derive (Debug , Clone)] pub struct PhylumReport { # [doc = " Whether the whole phylum checked clean."] pub ok : bool , # [doc = " How many sources were assembled into the phylum."] pub files_checked : usize , # [doc = " The single blocking refusal, if any (`None` iff `ok`)."] pub error : Option < PhylumError > , # [doc = " One verdict per nodule (P-A, DN-124 §2.3) — populated whenever the phylum was successfully"] # [doc = " **assembled** (every source parsed, no duplicate `nodule` paths); left empty on a `Parse`/"] # [doc = " `Duplicate` refusal (nodule identity itself is ambiguous/unknown there — no partial credit is"] # [doc = " attempted, VR-5). **Never conflated with `ok`**: `ok`/`error` stay the whole-phylum verdict"] # [doc = " unchanged; `nodules` is the additional, strictly-more-informative partial view. A reader can"] # [doc = " never mistake \"k nodules Clean\" for \"the phylum builds\" — both signals are always present."] pub nodules : Vec < NoduleVerdict > , }

gaps.snippet:
# [doc = " Check an explicit set of `(path, contents)` sources **as one cross-resolving phylum** (the"] # [doc = " FS-free core, mirroring [`check_sources`]'s shape so it is unit-testable). See the module note"] # [doc = " above for the honesty contract."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Steps: parse each source (any parse failure → an explicit `Parse` refusal — the phylum cannot be"] # [doc = " assembled), guard against duplicate `nodule` paths (a `Duplicate` refusal — never a silent export"] # [doc = " collision, G2), assemble one `Phylum { path: None, .. }`, and run [`mycelium_l1::check_phylum`]"] # [doc = " (all-or-nothing: `Clean` verdicts on success, one faithful `Check` refusal on failure)."] # [must_use] pub fn check_phylum_sources (sources : & [(String , String)]) -> PhylumReport { let files_checked = sources . len () ; let mut nodules : Vec < mycelium_l1 :: Nodule > = Vec :: with_capacity (files_checked) ; let mut files : Vec < String > = Vec :: with_capacity (files_checked) ; for (file , src) in sources { match parse (src) { Ok (nodule) => { nodules . push (nodule) ; files . push (file . clone ()) ; } Err (e) => { return PhylumReport { ok : false , files_checked , error : Some (PhylumError { kind : PhylumErrorKind :: Parse , site : file . clone () , message : e . to_string () , }) , nodules : Vec :: new () , } ; } } } let mut seen : std :: collections :: BTreeSet < String > = std :: collections :: BTreeSet :: new () ; for nodule in & nodules { let key = nodule . path . 0 . join (".") ; if ! seen . insert (key . clone ()) { return PhylumReport { ok : false , files_checked , error : Some (PhylumError { kind : PhylumErrorKind :: Duplicate , site : key . clone () , message : format ! ("nodule `{key}` is declared more than once — every nodule path in a phylum \
                         must be unique") , }) , nodules : Vec :: new () , } ; } } let phylum = mycelium_l1 :: Phylum { path : None , nodules , } ; match mycelium_l1 :: check_phylum (& phylum) { Ok (_) => { let verdicts = phylum . nodules . iter () . zip (files . iter ()) . map (| (n , file) | NoduleVerdict { nodule : n . path . 0 . join (".") , file : file . clone () , class : NoduleClass :: Clean , }) . collect () ; PhylumReport { ok : true , files_checked , error : None , nodules : verdicts , } } Err (ce) => { let partial = compute_partial_verdicts (& phylum . nodules , & files) ; PhylumReport { ok : false , files_checked , error : Some (PhylumError { kind : PhylumErrorKind :: Check , site : ce . site , message : ce . message , }) , nodules : partial , } } } }

Emitting bare `len(recv, …)` would fabricate an unknown prim and file-poison myc-check (G-β Rank A / G2/VR-5; residual when the method body itself gapped — e.g. M-1037 `.to_vec()` inside `read_to_end` — so the declaration was never registered)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Build the intra-phylum `use` **edge relation**: for each nodule (keyed by its dotted path), the"] # [doc = " dotted paths of the OTHER in-batch nodules its `use`s target. Mirrors exactly the target-path"] # [doc = " arithmetic [`mycelium_l1`]'s own `resolve_imports` uses (a specific `use a.b.Item` targets nodule"] # [doc = " `a.b`; a glob `use a.b.*` targets nodule `a.b`) — so an edge here means \"this is the nodule"] # [doc = " `check_phylum`'s own resolver would look up for this `use`\", not a guess."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Cross-phylum `use dep::…` references ([`UsePath::phylum`] `Some`) contribute **no** edge (DN-113/"] # [doc = " M-1060, DN-124 OQ-2 — out of scope for the driver-level MVP; a phylum whose only uses are"] # [doc = " cross-phylum degrades to each nodule's closure being itself, the conservative default: it cannot"] # [doc = " resolve such a use locally either way, so this can only under-credit, never over-credit)."] fn build_use_edges (nodules : & [mycelium_l1 :: Nodule]) -> BTreeMap < String , Vec < String > > { let mut edges = BTreeMap :: new () ; for n in nodules { let mut targets = Vec :: new () ; for item in & n . items { if let Item :: Use (UsePath { phylum : None , path , glob , }) = item { let target = if * glob { path . 0 . join (".") } else if path . 0 . len () > 1 { path . 0 [.. path . 0 . len () - 1] . join (".") } else { continue ; } ; targets . push (target) ; } } edges . insert (n . path . 0 . join (".") , targets) ; } edges }
gaps.reason: qualified type path `mycelium_l1 :: Nodule` — collapsing to its last segment would risk colliding with an unrelated same-named local type (e.g. `std::cmp::Ordering` vs a local `Ordering`); left an explicit gap rather than guessed (VR-5)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " *N*'s transitive in-batch import closure, `{N} ∪ closure(N)` (DN-124 §2.1/§2.2) — a BFS over"] # [doc = " `edges`, restricted to nodule paths actually **present** in this batch. A `use` target not present"] # [doc = " in the batch contributes no edge: it is not a false-clean risk (DN-124 §6 Attack 2) — it simply"] # [doc = " leaves that `use` unresolved when the sub-phylum is later checked, a conservative under-credit."] fn closure_of (start : & str , edges : & BTreeMap < String , Vec < String > > , present : & BTreeSet < String > ,) -> BTreeSet < String > { let mut visited : BTreeSet < String > = BTreeSet :: new () ; let mut queue : VecDeque < String > = VecDeque :: new () ; queue . push_back (start . to_owned ()) ; while let Some (cur) = queue . pop_front () { if ! visited . insert (cur . clone ()) { continue ; } if let Some (targets) = edges . get (& cur) { for t in targets { if present . contains (t) && ! visited . contains (t) { queue . push_back (t . clone ()) ; } } } } visited }
gaps.reason: `let` binding uses an unsupported pattern (only simple `let x = e;` is supported)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Attribute a [`mycelium_l1::CheckError`]'s `site` to the closure member it belongs to, when"] # [doc = " **cleanly recoverable**: most check-error sites are bare item names (a fn/type/trait name, never"] # [doc = " nodule-qualified — see `check_fn_body`/`register_nodule_decls` in `mycelium-l1`), so this can only"] # [doc = " confidently attribute the small set of sites the kernel *does* nodule-qualify (`<use>`/"] # [doc = " `<totality>`, via its own `qualify(&nodule.path, …)`). Picks the **longest** matching nodule-path"] # [doc = " prefix (the most specific enclosing nodule, for a batch with nested paths like `a` and `a.b`)."] # [doc = " Returns `None` when no closure member's path is a prefix of `site` — the honest \"not cleanly"] # [doc = " recoverable\" case (VR-5: [`compute_partial_verdicts`] falls back to the weaker `CheckError` there,"] # [doc = " never guesses `Blocked`)."] fn site_owner (site : & str , closure_paths : & BTreeSet < String >) -> Option < String > { closure_paths . iter () . filter (| p | ! p . is_empty () && (site == p . as_str () || site . starts_with (& format ! ("{p}.")))) . max_by_key (| p | p . len ()) . cloned () }
gaps.reason: method call `.cloned(...)` has no proven-emitted free-fn referent in this file (neither a D4-mangled inherent `Type__cloned` nor a bare inherent recorded by this file's left-to-right pass, nor a prim_map / combinator row). Emitting bare `cloned(recv, …)` would fabricate an unknown prim and file-poison myc-check (G-β Rank A / G2/VR-5; residual when the method body itself gapped — e.g. M-1037 `.to_vec()` inside `read_to_end` — so the declaration was never registered)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " P-A (DN-124 §2.2): sound partial per-nodule verdicts via a driver-level import-closure sub-phylum"] # [doc = " re-check that reuses [`mycelium_l1::check_phylum`] **unchanged** (KC-3/DRY — zero kernel growth)."] # [doc = " Called only when the whole phylum did **not** check clean (see the call site for why the clean"] # [doc = " case needs no re-check). `files` is index-parallel to `nodules` (see [`NoduleVerdict::file`])."] fn compute_partial_verdicts (nodules : & [mycelium_l1 :: Nodule] , files : & [String] ,) -> Vec < NoduleVerdict > { let edges = build_use_edges (nodules) ; let present : BTreeSet < String > = nodules . iter () . map (| n | n . path . 0 . join (".")) . collect () ; nodules . iter () . zip (files . iter ()) . map (| (n , file) | { let n_path = n . path . 0 . join (".") ; let closure = closure_of (& n_path , & edges , & present) ; let sub_nodules : Vec < mycelium_l1 :: Nodule > = nodules . iter () . filter (| m | closure . contains (& m . path . 0 . join ("."))) . cloned () . collect () ; let sub_phylum = mycelium_l1 :: Phylum { path : None , nodules : sub_nodules , } ; let class = match mycelium_l1 :: check_phylum (& sub_phylum) { Ok (_) => NoduleClass :: Clean , Err (ce) => match site_owner (& ce . site , & closure) { Some (owner) if owner == n_path => NoduleClass :: CheckError { site : ce . site , message : ce . message , } , Some (owner) => NoduleClass :: Blocked { on : owner , message : ce . message , } , None => NoduleClass :: CheckError { site : ce . site , message : ce . message , } , } , } ; NoduleVerdict { nodule : n_path , file : file . clone () , class , } }) . collect () }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Resolve and check every `.myc` under `dir` **as one phylum** (the FS wrapper over"] # [doc = " [`check_phylum_sources`], mirroring [`check_project`]'s discovery/read path)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = " [`ResolveError`] when there are no `.myc` sources, or a source cannot be read (missing/ambiguous"] # [doc = " input; G2 — never a silent empty pass)."] pub fn check_phylum_dir (dir : & Path) -> Result < PhylumReport , ResolveError > { let files = collect_myc (dir) ? ; if files . is_empty () { return Err (ResolveError (format ! ("no `.myc` sources under {} — nothing to check (a clean exit here would be a silent pass; G2)" , dir . display ()))) ; } let mut sources = Vec :: with_capacity (files . len ()) ; for f in files { let src = std :: fs :: read_to_string (& f) . map_err (| e | ResolveError (format ! ("{}: {e}" , f . display ()))) ? ; let rel = f . strip_prefix (dir) . unwrap_or (& f) . to_string_lossy () . replace ('\\' , "/") ; sources . push ((rel , src)) ; } Ok (check_phylum_sources (& sources)) }
gaps.reason: unsupported expression form `collect_myc (dir) ?`

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Collect every `.myc` under `dir` (recursively), sorted; skipping hidden entries and `target/`."] fn collect_myc (dir : & Path) -> Result < Vec < PathBuf > , ResolveError > { let mut out = Vec :: new () ; walk (dir , & mut out) ? ; out . sort () ; Ok (out) }
gaps.reason: qualified/associated-function call `Vec :: new` did not resolve to a known-emitted associated fn — Mycelium calls are bare identifiers, and the only sound surface form for `Type::method(...)` is the mangled `Type__method` name the declaration side already emits for a receiver-less inherent-impl associated fn (DN-34 §8.13/8.14), referenced only when that exact declaration is PROVEN present (same-file, or a resolved M-1084 cross-nodule sibling). A primitive/std associated fn with no emitted decl (e.g. `i128::try_from`), an unresolved type, or a `self`-receiving method (excluded by construction — it stays reachable only from its own bare call site) all gap here rather than fabricate a call (G2/VR-5, DN-133)

gaps.snippet: fn walk (dir : & Path , out : & mut Vec < PathBuf >) -> Result < () , ResolveError > { let entries = std :: fs :: read_dir (dir) . map_err (| e | ResolveError (format ! ("{}: {e}" , dir . display ()))) ? ; let mut paths : Vec < PathBuf > = entries . filter_map (| e | e . ok () . map (| e | e . path ())) . collect () ; paths . sort () ; for path in paths { let name = path . file_name () . and_then (| s | s . to_str ()) . unwrap_or_default () ; if name . starts_with ('.') || name == "target" { continue ; } if path . is_dir () { walk (& path , out) ? ; } else if path . extension () . is_some_and (| x | x == "myc") { out . push (path) ; } } Ok (()) }
gaps.reason: unsupported expression form `std :: fs :: read_dir (dir) . map_err (| e | ResolveError (format ! ("{}: {e}" , dir . display ()))) ?`
